zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey II de Saye於1155年出生在英國肯特韋斯特 格林維其時，他的父親Geoffrey de Saye I21歲，母親Alice de Cheyne16歲。他於1206年與Hawise de Clare在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1230年8月19日在法國波特芝士享年75歲，埋葬在英國肯特多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey II de Saye于1155年出生在英国肯特韋斯特 格林維其时，他的父亲Geoffrey de Saye I21岁，母亲Alice de Cheyne16岁。他于1206年与Hawise de Clare在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1230年8月19日在法国波特芝士享年75岁，埋葬在英国肯特多福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey II de Saye was born in 1155, in West Greenwich, Kent, England, his father, Geoffrey de Saye I, was 21 and his mother, Alice de Cheyne, was 16. He married Hawise de Clare in 1206, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 19 August 1230, in Poitou, France, at the age of 75, and was buried in Dover, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey II de Saye est né en 1155, à West Greenwich, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Geoffrey de Saye I, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Alice de Cheyne, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Hawise de Clare en 1206, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 19 août 1230, à Poitou, France, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Dover, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey II de Saye 1155 in West Greenwich, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de Saye I 21 und seine Mutter Alice de Cheyne 16. Er hat 1206 in Suffolk, England Hawise de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 19. August 1230 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Poitou, Frankreich und wurde in Dover, Kent, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey II de Sayeが1155年にイギリスケントウエスト グレエンウィチで生まれた時，父Geoffrey de Saye Iは21歳，母Alice de Cheyneは16歳であった。1206年にイギリススフォルクでHawise de Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1230年8月19日にフランスポイトウで75歳で死去し，イギリスケントドバーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey II de Saye(이)가 1155년 영국 켄트 웨스트 그린위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de Saye I(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Cheyne(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1206년 영국 수폴크에서 Hawise de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1230년 8월 19일 프랑스 포이토우에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 도버에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Hawise de Clare em 1206, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey II de Saye родился в 1155 г. в местности вест гринвич, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Geoffrey de Saye I было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Alice de Cheyne, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise de Clare в 1206 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 19 августа 1230 г. в местности поитоу, Франция в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности довир, кэнт, Англия.
